info.title: Lumera Cosmos EVM JSON-RPC API
info.description: Auto-generated method catalog from Cosmos EVM JSON-RPC namespace implementations.

methods.name: debug_blockProfile
methods.description: BlockProfile turns on goroutine profiling for nsec seconds and writes profile data to file. It uses a profile rate of 1 for most accurate information. If a different rate is desired, set the rate and write the profile manually.

examples.name: capture-block-profile
examples.summary: Starts block profiling for 5 seconds and writes to a pprof file.

methods.name: debug_cpuProfile
methods.description: CpuProfile turns on CPU profiling for nsec seconds and writes profile data to file.

examples.name: capture-cpu-profile
examples.summary: Captures CPU profile for 10 seconds.

methods.name: debug_freeOSMemory
methods.description: FreeOSMemory forces a garbage collection.

examples.name: trigger-gc-memory-release
examples.summary: Hints runtime to return memory to the OS.

methods.name: debug_gcStats
methods.description: GcStats returns GC statistics.

examples.name: gc-stats
examples.summary: Returns current Go GC statistics.

methods.name: debug_getBlockRlp
methods.description: GetBlockRlp retrieves the RLP encoded for of a single block.

examples.name: block-rlp-by-height
examples.summary: Returns RLP-encoded Ethereum block bytes.

methods.name: debug_getHeaderRlp
methods.description: GetHeaderRlp retrieves the RLP encoded for of a single header.

examples.name: header-rlp-by-height
examples.summary: Returns RLP-encoded Ethereum header bytes.

methods.name: debug_getRawBlock
methods.description: GetRawBlock retrieves the RLP-encoded block by block number or hash.

schema.description: Block number (hex) or block hash (0x-prefixed 32-byte hex), optionally with requireCanonical flag

examples.name: raw-block-latest
examples.summary: Returns RLP bytes for the latest block.

methods.name: debug_goTrace
methods.description: GoTrace turns on tracing for nsec seconds and writes trace data to file.

examples.name: capture-go-trace
examples.summary: Starts Go execution trace and writes to file for 3 seconds.

methods.name: debug_intermediateRoots
methods.description: IntermediateRoots executes a block, and returns a list of intermediate roots: the stateroot after each transaction.

examples.name: intermediate-state-roots
examples.summary: Returns intermediate state roots while replaying tx execution.

methods.name: debug_memStats
methods.description: MemStats returns detailed runtime memory statistics.

BySize.description: Go type: [61]struct { Size uint32; Mallocs uint64; Frees uint64 }

items.x-go-type: struct { Size uint32; Mallocs uint64; Frees uint64 }

BySize.x-go-type: [61]struct { Size uint32; Mallocs uint64; Frees uint64 }

examples.name: memory-stats
examples.summary: Returns runtime memory statistics.

methods.name: debug_mutexProfile
methods.description: MutexProfile turns on mutex profiling for nsec seconds and writes profile data to file. It uses a profile rate of 1 for most accurate information. If a different rate is desired, set the rate and write the profile manually.

examples.name: capture-mutex-profile
examples.summary: Captures mutex contention profile.

methods.name: debug_printBlock
methods.description: PrintBlock retrieves a block and returns its pretty printed form.

examples.name: print-block
examples.summary: Returns pretty-printed block dump by number.

methods.name: debug_setBlockProfileRate
methods.description: SetBlockProfileRate sets the rate of goroutine block profile data collection. rate 0 disables block profiling.

examples.name: set-block-rate
examples.summary: Enables block profiling with sample rate 1.

methods.name: debug_setGCPercent
methods.description: SetGCPercent sets the garbage collection target percentage. It returns the previous setting. A negative value disables GC.

examples.name: set-gc-percent
examples.summary: Sets GOGC threshold and returns previous value.

methods.name: debug_setMutexProfileFraction
methods.description: SetMutexProfileFraction sets the rate of mutex profiling.

examples.name: set-mutex-fraction
examples.summary: Sets mutex profiling fraction to 1.

methods.name: debug_stacks
methods.description: Stacks returns a printed representation of the stacks of all goroutines.

examples.name: goroutine-stacks
examples.summary: Returns current goroutine stack dump.

methods.name: debug_startCPUProfile
methods.description: StartCPUProfile turns on CPU profiling, writing to the given file.

examples.name: start-cpu-profile
examples.summary: Starts CPU profiling until debug_stopCPUProfile.

methods.name: debug_startGoTrace
methods.description: StartGoTrace turns on tracing, writing to the given file.

examples.name: start-go-trace
examples.summary: Starts Go tracing until debug_stopGoTrace.

methods.name: debug_stopCPUProfile
methods.description: StopCPUProfile stops an ongoing CPU profile.

examples.name: stop-cpu-profile
examples.summary: Stops active CPU profile and flushes output.

methods.name: debug_stopGoTrace
methods.description: StopGoTrace stops an ongoing trace.

examples.name: stop-go-trace
examples.summary: Stops active Go trace and flushes output.

methods.name: debug_traceBlock
methods.description: TraceBlock returns the structured logs created during the execution of EVM and returns them as a JSON object. It accepts an RLP-encoded block.

examples.name: trace-block-rlp
examples.summary: Traces all txs in an RLP-encoded block payload.

methods.name: debug_traceBlockByHash
methods.description: TraceBlockByHash returns the structured logs created during the execution of EVM and returns them as a JSON object.

examples.name: trace-block-by-hash
examples.summary: Traces all txs in a block selected by hash.

methods.name: debug_traceBlockByNumber
methods.description: TraceBlockByNumber returns the structured logs created during the execution of EVM and returns them as a JSON object.

schema.description: Block number: hex integer or tag ("latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", "finalized")

examples.name: trace-block-by-number
examples.summary: Traces all txs in a block selected by number/tag.

methods.name: debug_traceCall
methods.description: TraceCall lets you trace a given eth_call. It collects the structured logs created during the execution of EVM if the given transaction was added on top of the provided block and returns them as a JSON object.

schema.description: Arguments for message calls and transaction submission, using Ethereum JSON-RPC hex encoding. Use either legacy `gasPrice` or EIP-1559 fee fields. If you provide blob sidecar fields, provide `blobs`, `commitments`, and `proofs` together.

chainId.description: Chain ID this authorization is valid for.

nonce.description: Authorization nonce encoded as a hex uint64.

yParity.description: Signature y-parity encoded as a hex uint64.

items.description: EIP-4844 blob payload encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (131072 bytes).

chainId.description: Chain ID to sign against. If set, it must match the node chain ID.

items.description: EIP-4844 KZG commitment encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (48 bytes).

data.description: Legacy calldata field kept for backwards compatibility. Prefer `input`.

gas.description: Gas limit to use. If omitted, the node may estimate it.

gasPrice.description: Legacy gas price. Do not combine with EIP-1559 fee fields.

input.description: Preferred calldata field for contract calls and deployments.

maxFeePerBlobGas.description: EIP-4844 maximum fee per blob gas.

maxFeePerGas.description: EIP-1559 maximum total fee per gas.

maxPriorityFeePerGas.description: EIP-1559 maximum priority fee per gas.

nonce.description: Explicit sender nonce.

items.description: EIP-4844 KZG proof encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (48 bytes).

to.description: Recipient address. Omit for contract creation.

schema.description: Block number (hex) or block hash (0x-prefixed 32-byte hex), optionally with requireCanonical flag

examples.name: trace-eth-call
examples.summary: Traces an eth_call at a selected block.

methods.name: debug_traceTransaction
methods.description: TraceTransaction returns the structured logs created during the execution of EVM and returns them as a JSON object.

examples.name: trace-tx
examples.summary: Traces a single transaction by hash.

methods.name: debug_writeBlockProfile
methods.description: WriteBlockProfile writes a goroutine blocking profile to the given file.

examples.name: write-block-profile
examples.summary: Writes current block profile snapshot to disk.

methods.name: debug_writeMemProfile
methods.description: WriteMemProfile writes an allocation profile to the given file. Note that the profiling rate cannot be set through the API, it must be set on the command line.

examples.name: write-memory-profile
examples.summary: Writes current heap profile to disk.

methods.name: debug_writeMutexProfile
methods.description: WriteMutexProfile writes a goroutine blocking profile to the given file.

examples.name: write-mutex-profile
examples.summary: Writes current mutex profile to disk.

methods.description: Accounts returns the list of accounts available to this node.

methods.name: eth_call
methods.description: Call performs a raw contract call.

schema.description: Arguments for message calls and transaction submission, using Ethereum JSON-RPC hex encoding. Use either legacy `gasPrice` or EIP-1559 fee fields. If you provide blob sidecar fields, provide `blobs`, `commitments`, and `proofs` together.

chainId.description: Chain ID this authorization is valid for.

chainId.description: Chain ID to sign against. If set, it must match the node chain ID.

data.description: Legacy calldata field kept for backwards compatibility. Prefer `input`.

gas.description: Gas limit to use. If omitted, the node may estimate it.

params.name: overrides
params.description: Optional ephemeral state overrides applied only while executing this call.

additionalProperties.description: Account override applied during eth_call or access-list generation. Use either `state` to replace storage entirely or `stateDiff` to patch individual slots.

balance.description: Override the account balance for this call.

code.description: Override the account bytecode for this call.

movePrecompileToAddress.description: Move a precompile to this address for the duration of the call.

nonce.description: Override the account nonce for this call.

additionalProperties.description: Override value for this storage slot.

state.description: Replace the full storage map for this account during the call.

additionalProperties.description: Override value for this storage slot.

stateDiff.description: Patch only the listed storage slots during the call.

schema.description: Optional ephemeral account state overrides applied only while executing the call. Each top-level key is an account address.

examples.name: erc20-balance-of
examples.summary: Calls `balanceOf(address)` against an ERC-20 contract at the latest block.

methods.name: eth_chainId
methods.description: ChainId is the EIP-155 replay-protection chain id for the current ethereum chain config.

examples.name: chain-id
examples.summary: Returns the configured EVM chain ID in hex.

methods.name: eth_createAccessList
methods.description: CreateAccessList returns the list of addresses and storage keys used by the transaction (except for the sender account and precompiles), plus the estimated gas if the access list were added to the transaction.

methods.name: eth_fillTransaction
methods.description: FillTransaction fills the defaults (nonce, gas, gasPrice or 1559 fields) on a given unsigned transaction, and returns it to the caller for further processing (signing + broadcast).

methods.name: eth_getFilterChanges
methods.description: GetFilterChanges returns the logs for the filter with the given id since last time it was called. This can be used for polling. For pending transaction and block filters the result is []common.Hash. (pending)Log filters return []Log. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getfilterchanges

examples.name: poll-filter
examples.summary: Returns new entries since last poll for a filter id.

methods.name: eth_getFilterLogs
methods.description: GetFilterLogs returns the logs for the filter with the given id. If the filter could not be found an empty array of logs is returned. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getfilterlogs

methods.name: eth_getHeaderByNumber
methods.description: GetHeaderByNumber returns the requested canonical block header. - When blockNr is -1 the chain pending header is returned. - When blockNr is -2 the chain latest header is returned. - When blockNr is -3 the chain finalized header is returned. - When blockNr is -4 the chain safe header is returned.

schema.description: Block number: hex integer or tag ("latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", "finalized")

methods.name: eth_getLogs
methods.description: GetLogs returns logs matching the given argument that are stored within the state. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getlogs

schema.description: Log filter query used by eth_getLogs and filter subscription methods. Use either `blockHash` or a `fromBlock`/`toBlock` range.

address.description: Single contract address or array of addresses to match.

oneOf.description: One or more contract addresses to match.

blockHash.description: Restrict results to a single block hash. Mutually exclusive with fromBlock/toBlock.

fromBlock.description: Start of the block range, inclusive. Use a hex block number or one of "latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", or "finalized".

toBlock.description: End of the block range, inclusive. Use a hex block number or one of "latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", or "finalized".

topics.description: Up to four topic filters. Each position is AND-matched; nested arrays are OR-matched within a position; null means wildcard.

oneOf.description: Single topic hash to match at this position.
OR-match any of these topic hashes at this position.

examples.name: range-query
examples.summary: Returns logs in a bounded block range (can be empty).

methods.name: eth_newBlockFilter
methods.description: NewBlockFilter creates a filter that fetches blocks that are imported into the chain. It is part of the filter package since polling goes with eth_getFilterChanges. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_newblockfilter

examples.name: create-block-filter
examples.summary: Creates a block filter and returns filter id.

methods.name: eth_newFilter
methods.description: NewFilter creates a new filter and returns the filter id. It can be used to retrieve logs when the state changes. This method cannot be used to fetch logs that are already stored in the state. Default criteria for the from and to block are "latest". Using "latest" as block number will return logs for mined blocks. Using "pending" as block number returns logs for not yet mined (pending) blocks. In case logs are removed (chain reorg) previously returned logs are returned again but with the removed property set to true. In case "fromBlock" > "toBlock" an error is returned. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_newfilter

schema.description: Log filter query used by eth_getLogs and filter subscription methods. Use either `blockHash` or a `fromBlock`/`toBlock` range.

address.description: Single contract address or array of addresses to match.

oneOf.description: One or more contract addresses to match.

blockHash.description: Restrict results to a single block hash. Mutually exclusive with fromBlock/toBlock.

fromBlock.description: Start of the block range, inclusive. Use a hex block number or one of "latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", or "finalized".

toBlock.description: End of the block range, inclusive. Use a hex block number or one of "latest", "earliest", "pending", "safe", or "finalized".

topics.description: Up to four topic filters. Each position is AND-matched; nested arrays are OR-matched within a position; null means wildcard.

oneOf.description: Single topic hash to match at this position.
OR-match any of these topic hashes at this position.

methods.name: eth_newPendingTransactionFilter
methods.description: NewPendingTransactionFilter creates a filter that fetches pending transaction hashes as transactions enter the pending state. It is part of the filter package because this filter can be used through the `eth_getFilterChanges` polling method that is also used for log filters. https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_newPendingTransactionFilter

methods.name: eth_resend
methods.description: Resend accepts an existing transaction and a new gas price and limit. It will remove the given transaction from the pool and reinsert it with the new gas price and limit.

schema.description: Arguments for message calls and transaction submission, using Ethereum JSON-RPC hex encoding. Use either legacy `gasPrice` or EIP-1559 fee fields. If you provide blob sidecar fields, provide `blobs`, `commitments`, and `proofs` together.

chainId.description: Chain ID this authorization is valid for.

nonce.description: Authorization nonce encoded as a hex uint64.

yParity.description: Signature y-parity encoded as a hex uint64.

items.description: EIP-4844 blob payload encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (131072 bytes).

items.description: EIP-4844 KZG commitment encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (48 bytes).

data.description: Legacy calldata field kept for backwards compatibility. Prefer `input`.

gas.description: Gas limit to use. If omitted, the node may estimate it.

gasPrice.description: Legacy gas price. Do not combine with EIP-1559 fee fields.

input.description: Preferred calldata field for contract calls and deployments.

maxFeePerBlobGas.description: EIP-4844 maximum fee per blob gas.

maxFeePerGas.description: EIP-1559 maximum total fee per gas.

maxPriorityFeePerGas.description: EIP-1559 maximum priority fee per gas.

nonce.description: Explicit sender nonce.

items.description: EIP-4844 KZG proof encoded as 0x-prefixed hex (48 bytes).

to.description: Recipient address. Omit for contract creation.

methods.name: eth_syncing
methods.description: Syncing returns false in case the node is currently not syncing with the network. It can be up to date or has not yet received the latest block headers from its pears. In case it is synchronizing: - startingBlock: block number this node started to synchronize from - currentBlock: block number this node is currently importing - highestBlock: block number of the highest block header this node has received from peers - pulledStates: number of state entries processed until now - knownStates: number of known state entries that still need to be pulled

methods.name: miner_getHashrate
methods.description: GetHashrate returns the current hashrate for local CPU miner and remote miner. Unsupported in Cosmos EVM

examples.name: get-hashrate
examples.summary: Mining is unsupported in Cosmos EVM; hashrate is always zero.

methods.description: SetEtherbase sets the etherbase of the miner

examples.name: set-etherbase

methods.description: SetExtra sets the extra data string that is included when this miner mines a block. Unsupported in Cosmos EVM

examples.name: set-extra-data
examples.summary: Unsupported in Cosmos EVM; returns false and error.

methods.name: miner_setGasLimit
methods.description: SetGasLimit sets the gaslimit to target towards during mining. Unsupported in Cosmos EVM

examples.name: set-gas-limit
examples.summary: Unsupported in Cosmos EVM; returns false.

methods.name: miner_setGasPrice
methods.description: SetGasPrice sets the minimum accepted gas price for the miner.

examples.name: set-miner-gas-price
examples.summary: Updates miner-side minimum gas price.

methods.name: miner_start
methods.description: Start starts the miner with the given number of threads. If threads is nil, the number of workers started is equal to the number of logical CPUs that are usable by this process. If mining is already running, this method adjust the number of threads allowed to use and updates the minimum price required by the transaction pool. Unsupported in Cosmos EVM

examples.name: start-miner
examples.summary: Unsupported in Cosmos EVM; call returns an error.

methods.name: miner_stop
methods.description: Stop terminates the miner, both at the consensus engine level as well as at the block creation level. Unsupported in Cosmos EVM

examples.name: stop-miner
examples.summary: Unsupported in Cosmos EVM; no-op.

methods.description: Listening returns if client is actively listening for network connections.

methods.description: PeerCount returns the number of peers currently connected to the client.

examples.summary: Returns network ID as decimal string.

methods.name: personal_ecRecover
methods.description: EcRecover returns the address for the account that was used to create the signature. Note, this function is compatible with eth_sign and personal_sign. As such it recovers the address of: hash = keccak256("\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n"${message length}${message}) addr = ecrecover(hash, signature) Note, the signature must conform to the secp256k1 curve R, S and V values, where the V value must be 27 or 28 for legacy reasons. https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/wiki/Management-APIs#personal_ecRecove

examples.name: recover-address
examples.summary: Recovers signer from personal_sign-style payload/signature.

methods.name: personal_importRawKey
methods.description: ImportRawKey armors and encrypts a given raw hex encoded ECDSA key and stores it into the key directory. The name of the key will have the format "personal_<length-keys>", where <length-keys> is the total number of keys stored on the keyring. NOTE: The key will be both armored and encrypted using the same passphrase.

examples.name: import-private-key
examples.summary: Imports a raw secp256k1 private key into local keyring.

methods.name: personal_initializeWallet
methods.description: InitializeWallet initializes a new wallet at the provided URL, by generating and returning a new private key.

examples.name: initialize-smartcard-wallet
examples.summary: Smartcard wallets are currently unsupported; call returns an error.

methods.name: personal_listAccounts
methods.description: ListAccounts will return a list of addresses for accounts this node manages.

examples.name: list-local-accounts
examples.summary: Returns locally managed accounts in keyring.

methods.name: personal_listWallets
methods.description: ListWallets will return a list of wallets this node manages.

examples.name: list-wallets
examples.summary: Wallet-level management is not supported; returns null/empty.

methods.name: personal_lockAccount
methods.description: LockAccount will lock the account associated with the given address when it's unlocked. It removes the key corresponding to the given address from the API's local keys.

examples.name: lock-account
examples.summary: Lock/unlock via keyring backend is not supported; returns false.

methods.name: personal_newAccount
methods.description: NewAccount will create a new account and returns the address for the new account.

examples.name: create-account
examples.summary: Creates a new eth_secp256k1 account from mnemonic path iterator.

methods.name: personal_sendTransaction
methods.description: SendTransaction will create a transaction from the given arguments and tries to sign it with the key associated with args.To. If the given password isn't able to decrypt the key it fails.

methods.name: personal_sign
methods.description: Sign calculates an Ethereum ECDSA signature for: keccak256("\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n" + len(message) + message)) Note, the produced signature conforms to the secp256k1 curve R, S and V values, where the V value will be 27 or 28 for legacy reasons. The key used to calculate the signature is decrypted with the given password. https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/wiki/Management-APIs#personal_sign

examples.name: sign-message
examples.summary: Signs arbitrary bytes with Ethereum message prefixing.

methods.name: personal_unlockAccount
methods.description: UnlockAccount will unlock the account associated with the given address with the given password for duration seconds. If duration is nil it will use a default of 300 seconds. It returns an indication if the account was unlocked.

examples.name: unlock-account
examples.summary: Lock/unlock via keyring backend is not supported; returns false.

methods.description: Unpair deletes a pairing between wallet and Cosmos EVM.
